channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House unanimously passes bill to quadruple the pensions of Medal of Honor recipients
item.description: Legislation to quadruple pensions for Medal of Honor heroes passed the House Wednesday with strong bipartisan support and now goes to the United States Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: CNN pundit Scott Jennings encouraged to run for McConnell's open Senate seat
item.description: Scott Jennings, a conservative CNN pundit, is being privately and publicly encouraged to run for Kentucky Sen. Mitch McConnell’s open seat.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What Medicaid cuts could mean for Minnesota children
item.description: About one in five Minnesotans are on Medicaid. That shakes out to about one in six adults and three in 10 children.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: What's next for Trump agenda after House GOP approves tax breaks and slashed spending in budget
item.description: Now that House Republicans have passed an ambitious budget blueprint for President Donald Trump’s agenda, its time for hard work.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The National Archives is nonpartisan but has found itself targeted by Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As President Donald Trump moves to overhaul the federal government with astonishing speed, he has wreaked havoc on one agency long known for its nonpartisanship and revered for its mission: the National Archives and Records Administration.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Environmental Protection Agency eyeing to cut 65% of its staffers
item.description: President Donald Trump said that Environmental Protection Agency Administrator Lee Zeldin is eyeing cutting 65% of federal employees from the agency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bipartisan bill promises more resources at ports to fight fentanyl smuggling, speed up wait times
item.description: As drug smuggling attempts persist at ports of entry along the southern border, new bipartisan legislation seeks to make it easier for customs agents to effectively do their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Top blue-state Republican launches comprehensive DOGE effort, with a twist
item.description: Pennsylvania state Sen. Doug Mastriano is drafting plans to launch a DOGE committee in Harrisburg, and hopes the Democratic governor will go along.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: USAID employees given 15 minutes to clear out offices
item.description: U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees were notified late Tuesday they would be given 15 minutes to collect their belongings from their offices as the Trump administration forges ahead with gutting the agency.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada wants new oil pipelines to avoid Trump tariffs; nobody wants to build them
item.description: Canada sends 90 percent of oil exports to U.S. refiners. The Canadian government would have to play a significant role in any project to build new oil pipelines in Canada to overcome regulatory, financial and political hurdles and activist opposition, industry experts said.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: DOGE subcommittee looks at US foreign aid spending
item.description: Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene said the hearing comes as Americans are "shocked" to discover how their taxpayer dollars have been spent overseas.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump introduces a green card for the rich: the gold card
item.description: The new visa program would effectively replace the current EB-5 investor visa program, and the proceeds could be used for deficit reduction.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid, MPR News

item.title: Israel and Hamas agree on a new exchange, leaving a fragile ceasefire intact
item.description: The bodies of four Israeli hostages are to be returned late Wednesday and more than 600 Palestinian prisoners and detainees freed during the last week of the current Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin and Hadeel Al-Shalchi, MPR News

item.title: Judge gives Trump administration two days to release billions of dollars in blocked foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday gave the Trump administration less than two days to release billions of dollars in U.S. foreign aid, saying the administration had given no sign of complying with his nearly two-week-old court order to ease its funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Digital license plates allowed under proposal in the Nevada Legislature
item.description: A new bill at the Nevada Legislature would allow the DMV to accept digital license plates purchased from an approved vendor.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal employees may get more demands to justify their work at Elon Musk's direction
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The turmoil that enveloped the federal workforce over the last few days is unlikely to cease anytime soon as the U.S. government's human resources agency considers how to fulfill Elon Musk ’s demands.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bondi warns states to comply with Trump order on transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi warned officials in Maine, California and Minnesota on Tuesday to comply with President Trump’s executive orders to bar transgender athletes from participating in girls’ and women’s sports, promising swift legal action.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Social Security says higher payments are on the way for millions of former public workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 3.2 million Social Security recipients who received pensions from their time as teachers, firefighters, police officers and other public service jobs will soon see a boost in their benefits.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shutdown fight collides with GOP push for Trump priorities
item.description: The race to prevent a government shutdown next month is colliding with Republican efforts to enact President Trump’s tax and funding priorities. Congressional negotiators have been struggling for weeks to strike a bipartisan deal that would keep the government funded past a mid-March deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court throws out Oklahoma inmate Richard Glossip's murder conviction and death sentence
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Tuesday threw out the murder conviction and death penalty for Richard Glossip, an Oklahoma man who was found guilty in the killing of a motel owner but has steadfastly maintained his innocence and averted multiple attempts by the state to execute him.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House to take over press pool amid 'Gulf of America' fight with AP
item.description: The White House said Tuesday that it would take over which outlets are allowed into the press pool covering the president, wrestling such control from the White House Correspondents' Association.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Jon Stewart cuts hand during fiery monologue on DOGE, prescription drugs
item.description: Jon Stewart is railing against sweeping cuts by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) — and giving his hand one of its own in the process — during an impassioned monologue on "The Daily Show.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: AI video of Trump kissing Musk’s feet plays on TVs in HUD building
item.description: A video produced using artificial intelligence (AI) showing President Trump rubbing and kissing Elon Musk's feet played on television screens in the Department of Housing and Urban Development (HUD) building Monday in an apparent mocking of the relationship between the two men.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: 83 percent of Americans disapprove of Trump's Jan. 6 pardons: Poll
item.description: The latest poll also shows that more than half of Americans also do not approve of Trump's handling of the economy or his management of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis reappoints Moms for Liberty cofounder to Florida ethics commission
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis is attempting to put Moms for Liberty cofounder Tina Descovich on the Florida Commission on Ethics for the second time.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Former NFL player Chris Kluwe arrested while protesting MAGA sign at city council meeting
item.description: Former NFL punter Chris Kluwe was detained at a city council meeting in California on Tuesday after delivering an impassioned speech criticizing the design of a plaque to be installed at the Huntington Beach Central Library.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling controversial LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida Democrats file bill to repeal 6-week abortion ban
item.description: House Minority Leader Fentrice Driskell, who represents families in Hillsborough County, said the legislation comes because it’s a mandate from voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York rep. says Trump has committed 'dozens of impeachable offenses' so far
item.description: “Donald Trump has, in his first month, probably committed dozens of impeachable offenses,” Rep. Dan Goldman (D-N.Y.) said in a recent interview.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom proposes $125M mortgage relief program to help victims of recent natural disasters
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday proposed a new $125 million package to help residents whose homes were destroyed or damaged by recent natural disasters, including the recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
